title: Dangerous-plant vocabulary term
description: One toxic plant/fungus that a medicinal plant can be confused with. vocabularies/dangerous_plants.yaml is a list of these; each is referenced by id from a medicinal plant's dangerous_lookalikes[]. Canonical toxicology lives here ONCE.

id.description: Stable slug, e.g. conium-maculatum, veratrum-album, galerina-marginata.

kingdom.description: So the tool can label fungal lookalikes correctly.

toxic_principle.description: The toxin(s), e.g. 'coniine and γ-coniceine (piperidine alkaloids)'.

toxicity.description: Plain-English, consumer-safe description of the harm, e.g. 'Ingestion causes ascending paralysis and can be rapidly fatal.'

toxicity_category.description: Broad grouping: neurotoxic | cardiotoxic | cytotoxic | dermatotoxic | gastrointestinal | mixed.

onset.description: Optional but important for delayed-onset toxins (e.g. amatoxins: '6–24 h delayed; deceptively symptom-free early').

default_severity.description: Worst-case severity if ingested; a relation may narrow it per medicinal plant/part.

external_ids.description: Botanical/mycological identity anchors (same idea as plant.external_ids).

references.description: Sources for this toxic plant's identity + toxicology.

editor_notes.description: EDITOR-FACING ONLY. Never emitted to public build output.
